readable_scopes: learner's full user filesystem (read side, conventional CLI process)
env vars: HOME, SHELLTUTOR_HOME, NO_COLOR, PATH
callable_capabilities: bash builtins (mkdir, touch, printf, echo, cat, clear, seq, rm)
principals.approval_policy: none required at runtime; contributor-side gates declared in AGENTS.md (see A-005)
principals.sandbox_model: filesystem-only; script confines its own writes to $SANDBOX. No kernel-level isolation (bash + POSIX userland constraint).

citations.evidence: SANDBOX="${SHELLTUTOR_HOME:-$HOME/.shelltutor}"
setup_sandbox mkdir/heredoc-write/seq-redirect/touch — all under $SANDBOX
save_progress writes only $PROGRESS_FILE
rm -f "$PROGRESS_FILE"
tutor must not write outside its own working directory

readable_scopes: learner's full user filesystem (real interactive bash)
writable_scopes_claimed: $SANDBOX only — implied by welcome-screen text 'you cannot break anything' (shelltutor:169)
writable_scopes_actual: learner's full user filesystem — cd .., rm, writes to $HOME, etc. all work
callable_externals: anything the learner's account can run (curl, wget, ssh, sudo if the learner has the password, vim, man, ...)
secrets_access: anything the learner's account can read (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, etc.) — script neither grants nor blocks
principals.approval_policy: none — learner-controlled bash; no agentic approval applies
bypass_modes: the entire subshell is a 'bypass' of the implied sandbox if read at the welcome-screen level
outbound_network_scope: the learner's normal network access; script neither grants nor blocks
principals.sandbox_model: filesystem-only at the directory layer: rcfile sets CWD (cd $SANDBOX) and HISTFILE; no restriction on subsequent commands.
principals.flags_note: Wide subshell scope is not declared in user-facing copy; the welcome-screen claim implies a tighter sandbox than the implementation provides. Not 'escalation' — the learner already had this authority before opening the tutor.
principals.remediation_constraint: Strengthening enforcement (bwrap/firejail/chroot/unshare/sandbox-exec) violates AGENTS.md:75-78 bash-only dependency rule. Remediation path is narrowing the welcome-screen claim to match implementation, not the reverse.

citations.evidence: practice() spawns bash --rcfile <(...) -i < /dev/tty with cd $SANDBOX + HISTFILE + PS1 + navigation overrides
cd "$SANDBOX"
HISTFILE=$SANDBOX/.shelltutor_history; HISTSIZE=2000; HISTFILESIZE=4000; history -r; PROMPT_COMMAND='history -a'
next/prev/show/quit/exit overrides
Everything happens in ~/.shelltutor; you cannot break anything
SANDBOX="${SHELLTUTOR_HOME:-$HOME/.shelltutor}"

readable_scopes: same as A-002 once inside practice subshell

citations.evidence: welcome heredoc addressed to the learner

principals.approval_policy: human-in-loop for sensitive work; blocked for restricted work

approval_gates.tool_or_action: introduce dependency outside bash + POSIX userland

principals.sandbox_model: AGENTS.md-declared repo boundary; coding agents pause and ask before traversing.
principals.enforcement_layer: norms (CONTRIBUTING.md:72-75 'local commit discipline is the enforcement layer'); no pre-commit hook, no CI, no policy-as-code.

citations.evidence: Stay inside .../shelltutor for normal work. Reading or modifying material outside this repository requires explicit user scope expansion.
Change the tutor's safety surface ... only when the user request clearly calls for it.
Do not introduce dependencies that require installation outside bash and a standard POSIX userland
local commit discipline is the enforcement layer

cross_cutting_observations.title: Safety rules have no code-level enforcement
cross_cutting_observations.description: AGENTS.md safety rules (no privilege, no network, no writes outside $SANDBOX) are honored by current code but have no pre-commit hook, no CI scan, no fitness function. Norm-based enforcement (CONTRIBUTING.md:72-75) is the current layer.

citations.evidence: tutor must not write outside its own working directory, must not request elevated privileges, and must not reach the network
local commit discipline is the enforcement layer

cross_cutting_observations.title: Subshell scope claim vs implementation
cross_cutting_observations.description: A-002's actual filesystem scope is the learner's full user authority; user-facing claim at shelltutor:169 implies narrower sandbox. Remediation path consistent with project constraints is narrowing the claim (not strengthening enforcement, which would require non-bash dependencies forbidden by AGENTS.md:75-78).

citations.evidence: you cannot break anything
practice() — narrow rcfile-level sandboxing
Do not introduce dependencies that require installation outside bash and a standard POSIX userland
